prevalence.trend: stable with increasing recognition due to improved diagnostics

burden.dalyPerCapita: High - leading cause of disability in adults <50

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: No routine screening; diagnosis primarily through clinical assessment in primary care

regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Acute: triptans, NSAIDs; Prophylaxis: beta-blockers, topiramate, CGRP mAbs (erenumab, fremanezumab); EHF guidelines
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High disability burden, undertreatment despite available therapies
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Wide access to triptans and CGRP inhibitors, specialized headache centers, EHF consensus guidelines
